description: This is a hotel booking detail page on Booking.com showing The Cumberland hotel in London. The page displays hotel information including location, amenities, guest reviews (8.1 rating from 14,252 reviews), and reservation options for March 27-28. The interface includes tabbed navigation for different sections like Overview, Info & prices, Amenities, and Guest reviews, along with prominent Reserve and Search buttons for booking functionality.

This is Booking.com's flight search interface featuring a prominent search form with options for round-trip, one-way, and multi-city flights. The page includes input fields for departure/arrival locations, travel dates, and passenger count, along with flight type preferences. Below the main search area, there's an offers section highlighting exclusive deals and partner airlines, with the overall design emphasizing ease of flight comparison and booking.

This is a travel blog or content section on Booking.com showing articles written by Ben Taylor. The page displays travel-related articles with featured images, publication dates, and brief descriptions. Articles focus on vacation destinations in the USA, including romantic getaways and dog-friendly locations. The layout includes pagination controls and an editorial team section at the bottom.

This is a hotel booking website homepage displaying the user's recent search history for destinations like Paris, Hell's Kitchen, and New York State. The main content area features a section for last-minute hotel deals near the user's location, showcasing various hotels with images, names, ratings, and brief descriptions. The layout uses a card-based design with hotel images and key information to facilitate easy browsing and booking decisions.

This is a hotel booking website showing search results in a grid layout. Each hotel is displayed as a card with an image, name, location, rating, brief description, and pricing starting from a nightly rate. The interface includes standard booking site elements like ratings, review counts, and 'Show more' buttons for additional details. There's also a section for 'Last minute hotels near you this weekend' at the bottom, suggesting personalized recommendations.

This is a hotel booking search page on Booking.com specifically for hotels near San Francisco International Airport. The page features a search form with location autocomplete showing New York-related suggestions, date selection fields, and guest/room options. The interface includes navigation tabs for different travel services like flights, car rental, and attractions, along with a prominent search functionality for finding accommodations.

This is a hotel booking form page on Booking.com where users can specify additional requirements for their reservation. The form includes sections for estimated arrival time selection and crib/extra bed requests for an Executive Twin room. It displays clear policy information about extra bed availability and charges, with options to add a free crib or extra bed with age selection. The page includes navigation buttons to proceed to final booking details.

This is a Booking.com search results page specifically focused on pet-friendly hotels. The page displays various destination options in a card-based grid layout, featuring images of different locations like Mexico, Spain, and the United States. Each card contains destination images, titles, and brief descriptions to help users plan their pet-friendly travel. The layout includes a sidebar with additional related suggestions and a comprehensive footer with various travel-related links and options.

This is a search results page for New York attractions and activities on Booking.com. The interface features a filter sidebar on the left with location and category options, while the main content area displays attraction listings in a card format. Each result card includes an image, title, description, rating, price, and availability information with prominent 'See availability' buttons.

This is Booking.com's apartment search page featuring a prominent search form overlaid on a background image of apartment interiors. The page includes navigation tabs for different accommodation types, location and date input fields, guest selection options, and a section for recent searches. The interface uses a blue color scheme with clear call-to-action buttons and maintains the typical booking platform layout with user account access in the top right corner.

This is a travel booking website's landing page featuring flight search functionality. The page prominently displays a credit card rewards promotion for earning travel credits on flights, followed by a section showcasing various travel benefits like discounts on stays and rental cars. The bottom section highlights key selling points including wide selection, transparent pricing, and flexible booking options with accompanying icons and descriptive text.

This is Booking.com's flight search interface featuring a prominent search form for finding flights. The page includes options for round-trip, one-way, and multi-city trips, with fields for departure/arrival locations, travel dates, and passenger count. There's an important travel update notification about Middle East flight disruptions and longer wait times for customer service.

This is a travel booking website showing a search interface for New York vacations. The page features a prominent search form at the top with destination, date, and guest selection fields, followed by promotional content including vacation packages. Below are travel offer cards displaying flights from Los Angeles to New York and hotel accommodations with pricing, ratings, and imagery. The layout uses a card-based design to showcase different travel options with clear pricing and booking information.

This is a car rental booking page on Booking.com's Avis section. The interface features a prominent search form with pickup location, date selection using a dual-month calendar picker showing April and May 2026, and time selection. Below the search area is a carousel displaying available vehicle options including premium, medium, large, and small car categories. The page uses Booking.com's characteristic blue branding with a clean, travel-focused design optimized for rental car bookings.

This is Costco's website homepage featuring their typical warehouse club retail layout. The page displays a prominent promotional banner for furniture, main navigation categories like Grocery, Pharmacy, and Photo, and a sidebar showing grocery subcategories. The layout includes delivery method options and promotional tiles highlighting different shopping features. The interface uses Costco's signature red and blue branding with a clean, organized structure typical of large retail websites.

This is an access denied error page that appears when a user attempts to visit a restricted Costco webpage for grocery and household items. The page displays a clear error message stating the user lacks permission to access the requested resource. It includes a reference number for troubleshooting purposes and follows a standard, minimal error page layout with basic text information.

This is an Etsy product browsing page showing a grid layout of handmade jewelry and accessories. The page displays product cards with images, titles, and pricing information, featuring items like pearl necklaces, bracelets, and evening bags. There's a 'Picks inspired by your shopping' section with personalized product recommendations displayed in a horizontal scrollable format.

This is a property settings page within HubSpot's CRM platform, specifically showing company properties configuration. The interface allows users to manage, create, and configure data properties that collect information about companies in their database. The page features a comprehensive data table with filtering options, search functionality, and tabs for organizing different types of properties and settings. Users can view property details like name, group, creator, and usage, with options to create new properties and manage data quality monitoring.

This is a HubSpot login page displaying an authentication expiration notice. The page features a centered login form with email and password fields, a 'Remember me' checkbox, and forgot password option. A prominent error message indicates the user's authentication has expired due to inactivity and they need to log in again to continue. The page includes HubSpot branding and offers alternative sign-in methods along with a demo request option at the bottom.

This is a HubSpot landing pages management interface showing a data table of draft landing pages. The interface includes filtering options, search functionality, and a right-click context menu with various page actions like saving, printing, and translating. Users can manage multiple landing pages with status tracking and bulk operations available through the interface.

This is a dashboard creation interface within HubSpot that allows users to either create dashboards from scratch or select from pre-built templates. The page displays suggested templates like 'Marketing Channel Performance' and 'Website Visits & Engagements' with preview images, descriptions, and metadata about included reports. Users can search through available templates or start with a blank dashboard using the 'New dashboard' option at the top.

This is a user preferences settings page for HubSpot, a marketing automation and CRM platform. The interface shows various account settings including language preferences, date/time format, phone number configuration, and general working hours. The left sidebar provides navigation to different account management sections like integrations, security, and privacy settings, while the main content area displays form fields for personalizing the user experience.

This is a consent options configuration page within HubSpot's settings interface. The page allows users to edit external-facing content for consent options by language, currently showing German content. It includes sections for legitimate interest and privacy policy with editable text content in German, demonstrating GDPR compliance features for managing contact data processing consent.

A CRM deal creation modal overlaying a sales pipeline kanban board. The form contains fields for deal name, pipeline selection, deal stage, amount, and close date. The background shows a deal management interface with pipeline stages like 'Appointment Scheduled' and 'Decision Maker Bought-In', along with deal metrics and filtering options.

This is a user preferences settings page within HubSpot's CRM platform. The interface shows a two-level navigation structure with a sidebar containing grouped settings categories and a main content area with tabbed navigation for different preference types. The user is currently viewing their profile settings under the 'General' section, where they can edit personal information like name, profile image, and language preferences. The layout follows typical enterprise software patterns with clear visual hierarchy and organized information architecture.

This is a property logic configuration modal for a CRM system, specifically for editing Deal object properties. The interface allows users to set up conditional logic where properties are shown or hidden based on values in other properties. It features a controlling property selector on the left (Deal Priority Level) with conditional operators and values, while the right side shows a live preview of how the form will appear to users when the conditions are met.

This is a HubSpot property settings configuration page where users can manage contact and company properties. The interface shows a left sidebar with various settings categories and a main content area displaying property groups and configuration options. The page appears to be part of HubSpot's CRM system, allowing administrators to customize data fields and organize them into logical groups for better data management.

This is a property settings page within HubSpot's CRM platform, specifically showing deal property configuration. The interface displays a sidebar navigation for account management options and a main content area with tabs for Properties, Conditional logic, Groups, and Archived items. The current view shows property groups for deals, including Deal information, Deal Stage Properties, Deal activity, Analytics history, and Deal revenue, with the number of properties in each group displayed in a table format.

This is a HubSpot report creation interface that guides users through building reports. The page presents multiple pathways for report creation including template library, AI-generated reports, and custom reports. It uses a card-based layout to organize different options and includes helpful descriptions for each choice. The interface follows a wizard pattern with clear navigation and next steps to streamline the report building process.

This is a sales performance dashboard interface showing closed deals vs target metrics with a chart widget. The main dashboard area displays performance data while a modal overlay on the right allows users to add additional reports from a searchable library. The interface includes filtering options, quick filters, and advanced filters for data manipulation. Various AI-generated and custom reports are available for selection, indicating this is a comprehensive analytics platform for tracking sales and marketing performance.

A modal dialog for creating a new property group within what appears to be a CRM or business analytics platform. The dialog contains a text input field pre-filled with 'Company Financial Insights' as the group name, along with Save and Cancel action buttons. This interface allows users to organize custom properties for better data management and categorization of business objects.

This is a HubSpot sales performance dashboard for Q2 2024 in an empty state. The main content area displays an illustration of a chart icon with instructional text encouraging users to add reports and share data with their team. The interface includes standard dashboard controls like 'Manage dashboards' and 'Create dashboard' buttons, along with a link to educational resources about dashboards.

This is a property settings page within HubSpot's CRM platform. The interface shows a two-level navigation structure with account management options in the left sidebar and property configuration in the main content area. Users can manage different types of properties (subscription properties shown here) organized in groups, with options to view, edit, and delete property groups. The page displays property groups in a table format showing names and property counts, with tabs for different property categories like Properties, Conditional logic, Groups, and Archived items.

This is a website settings configuration page within HubSpot's content management system. The interface shows CSS & Stylesheets settings with options to add style sheets, and jQuery configuration options. The left sidebar contains a hierarchical navigation menu for various website management functions including Data Management, Tools, and other administrative sections. A prominent warning notification explains that HubSpot will no longer add new jQuery versions and recommends upgrading to the latest version.

This is a web page editor interface for creating landing pages, specifically showing the HubSpot page editor. The user is editing a summer sale promotional page with a large heading that reads 'Exclusive Summer Sale 2025. Save Big'. The interface includes editing tools in the left sidebar for modifying content and styling, with options for heading text, semantic heading levels, and other page properties. The main content area shows the live preview of the landing page being created.

This is a Salesforce CRM interface showing a contacts data table with recently viewed contacts. The page displays contact information in a structured table format with columns for name, account, phone, email, and contact owner. The interface includes typical CRM functionality like bulk actions (Import, Add to Campaign, Send Email), search capabilities, and a navigation sidebar with sections for different CRM modules like Sales, Marketing, and Service. The layout represents a standard contact management view within a business CRM system.

This is a modal dialog within a Salesforce CRM interface for sorting products in a price book. The modal allows users to drag and drop items to reorder them, with a single product 'DataVault Pro Analytics Suite' currently visible. The interface includes cancel and save buttons at the bottom, with instructions to drag items into the desired order before saving.

This is a modal dialog for creating a new opportunity in a CRM system. The form contains fields for opportunity details including amount, description, owner assignment, and sales pipeline status. It features dropdown menus for stage selection and forecast category with options like Pipeline, Best Case, Commit, and Closed. The interface shows typical CRM workflow elements with probability percentages and sales stage management.

This is a contact editing modal within a CRM system, showing form fields for managing contact information including account details, title, reporting structure, and contact information. The modal appears to be for editing a VP of Engineering contact at Acme Technologies Inc, with sections for basic info, description, and contact details like phone and email. The interface includes standard form elements and action buttons for saving or canceling changes.

This is a CRM account detail page for Acme Technologies Inc, showing comprehensive account information in collapsible sections including contact details, description, and ownership. The interface features a sidebar navigation menu, activity timeline with upcoming tasks and calls, and a new task creation modal with calendar date picker. The layout supports inline editing of account fields and displays related activities in an organized timeline format.

This is a contact creation modal dialog in a Salesforce CRM system. The form contains fields for basic contact information including phone, email, and a complete mailing address section with street, city, state/province, and postal code fields. The interface includes dropdown menus for location selection, an email opt-out checkbox, and standard form action buttons (Cancel, Save & New, Save) at the bottom.

This is a Salesforce CRM interface showing a leads data table with recently viewed contacts. The main table displays lead information including names, titles, companies, and phone numbers. An email composition modal is overlaid on top, allowing the user to send emails to selected leads with features like recipient management, subject line, and a rich text editor for the email body.

This is a Salesforce CRM opportunity detail page showing various sections including contact roles, files, and products. The interface displays a success message indicating an opportunity product record was updated. The page features expandable sections for different data types, a contact card for James Anderson with VP of Sales title, and a file upload area for document management.

This is an email template selection modal within a CRM system. The interface allows users to choose between contacts and leads, browse available email templates through a searchable table format, and filter by template folders. The modal displays template names, descriptions, and folder locations, with options for both public and private email templates specifically designed for customer onboarding and other business communications.

This is a Salesforce CRM interface showing a cases management page for Acme Technologies Inc. The page displays a data table with one case entry containing details like case number, subject, priority, date opened, status, and owner. There's a prominent notification banner suggesting to select 2 or 3 cases to try again, indicating some bulk action workflow. The interface includes typical CRM navigation elements and case management tools like merge cases, change owner, and filtering options.

This is a Salesforce CRM settings page specifically for configuring Activity Reminders. The page allows users to customize reminder preferences including task and event notifications in Lightning Experience, default reminder times, and email notification settings. A success message indicates that settings have been saved, and the interface provides various configuration options through checkboxes and dropdown menus. The left sidebar shows different settings categories while the main content area focuses on reminder-specific configurations.

This is a Salesforce CRM settings page for configuring fiscal year periods. The interface shows a multi-step configuration process where users can change their organization's fiscal year settings, with options for standard or custom fiscal years. The page includes warning notifications about the impact of changes on reporting and forecasting, and provides guidance for archiving current data before making modifications.

This is a Salesforce CRM administration settings page showing company information configuration. The interface features a left sidebar with various setup categories, a main content area displaying organization details like address, contact info, and system settings. Users can edit company profile information, manage licenses, and configure organizational preferences through this administrative interface.

This is a Salesforce setup page for configuring Event Relays, which allows sending platform events and change data capture events to Amazon EventBridge. The page shows an empty state indicating no Event Relays have been created yet, with a prominent 'New Event Relay' button to create one. The interface includes typical Salesforce setup navigation with a collapsible sidebar menu containing various platform tools and configuration options.

This is a Salesforce CRM settings page specifically for configuring domain settings. The interface shows a comprehensive admin panel with a left sidebar containing various configuration options organized under categories like Administration and Platform Tools. The main content area displays routing and policy settings with options for Salesforce Edge Network configuration, login policies, and cookie preferences. The page includes form controls like radio buttons and checkboxes for different configuration options.

This is a delete confirmation modal dialog within a CRM contact management system. The modal appears over a contacts list page and asks for confirmation before deleting a contact record. It features a clear warning message and two action buttons (Cancel and Delete) following standard destructive action confirmation patterns. The underlying page shows a 'Recently Viewed' contacts list with typical CRM navigation and data table structure.

This is a CRM account detail page for Acme Technologies Inc, showing company information and account management tools. The page features a sidebar navigation menu, account details in expandable sections, and a prominent 'New Event' modal dialog overlay for scheduling. The interface includes AI-powered features for productivity and customer insights, along with standard CRM functionality for managing business relationships and activities.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing a user's direct message conversation with themselves. The left sidebar contains navigation for channels, direct messages, and workspace tools, while the center shows the chat interface with a saved message. The right panel displays the user's profile information including contact details and status settings.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the direct messages section. The left sidebar displays navigation options and a list of DM conversations, while the right panel shows a user's bookmarks section with saved links. The interface includes typical messaging app features like search functionality, file sharing options, and notification management. A permission request banner appears at the bottom asking to enable notifications.

This is a custom emoji creation modal within Slack's messaging interface. Users can upload an image file and assign a name (like ':avocado:') to create custom emojis for their workspace. The modal includes file upload functionality, image preview, and form validation. The interface shows the typical Slack workspace structure in the background with direct messages and navigation sidebar.

This is a sharing dialog modal within Slack's canvas feature, allowing users to share a canvas document with team members or channels. The modal includes a search field to add people by name or channel, shows the current owner (jim snow), and provides advanced settings for limiting sharing permissions. At the bottom are options to copy a link or set invite-only permissions, with the modal overlaying the main Slack interface showing direct messages and canvas workspace.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the direct messages section. The left sidebar contains navigation for different sections like Home, DMs, Activity, Files, and Admin. The main content area displays a conversation thread between users, with options to share canvas documents and schedule messages. There's a search bar at the top filtering for a specific user, and the interface includes typical messaging features like text formatting tools and file attachment options.

This is a settings/preferences modal dialog within a file management application, specifically showing Salesforce integration options. The left sidebar contains various preference categories like Notifications, Navigation, Appearance, etc., while the main content area displays Salesforce org connection settings. The interface shows an empty state with a purple Salesforce logo, indicating no Salesforce organizations are currently connected. The modal appears to be part of a larger workspace or collaboration platform that allows users to integrate with external business applications.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the main messaging view with a sidebar containing channels, direct messages, and navigation options. A delete confirmation modal is prominently displayed in the center, asking the user to confirm deletion of a message from user 'Jim snow' with the text 'Hello @channel testing activity'. The interface includes typical Slack elements like channel threads, user profiles, and a notification permission request banner at the bottom.

This is a Slack-like team messaging application showing a social channel conversation. The interface displays a threaded conversation with messages, replies, and user mentions, along with a sidebar containing channels, direct messages, and other organizational features. The layout includes typical messaging app elements like user avatars, timestamps, and a message input area at the bottom.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the Directories section for team collaboration. The left sidebar contains navigation elements including channels, direct messages, and various workspace sections. A filter dropdown is open showing options to sort and filter conversations by activity, mentions, and other criteria. The main content area displays directory tiles and includes options for inviting people and managing workspace settings.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing a team communication platform. The left sidebar contains navigation for different channels, direct messages, and organizational features. The center area displays a conversation thread with messages from user 'jim snow', including test messages and canvas content. A user profile panel is open on the right side showing profile information, status settings, and notification preferences.

This is a Slack pricing page showing four subscription tiers (Free, Pro, Business+, Enterprise+) with their respective costs and features. The Business+ plan is highlighted as recommended, and each tier displays pricing, key features, and upgrade options. The page uses a clean layout with feature comparisons and clear call-to-action buttons for upgrading or contacting sales.

This is a file management interface for PlatoTest, a collaboration platform. The left sidebar shows file organization with sections for All files, Canvases, Lists, and Starred items, with an 'Untitled' file currently selected. The main content area displays a canvas creation interface with options to create new Canvas or List items, and shows a preview of an existing canvas with nature imagery titled 'Your canvas title'. A notification at the bottom requests permission to enable notifications from Slack integration.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the #all-platotest channel for team communication. The left sidebar contains navigation for channels, direct messages, and other workspace features, while the main area displays the chat conversation with timestamps and user messages. The interface includes typical messaging features like message composition, notifications, and the ability to star channels for quick access.

This is a sharing modal dialog within a collaboration platform that allows users to share a canvas/document with others. The interface shows user selection (Jim Sand), permission controls (Can view dropdown), direct messaging options, and notification settings. It appears to be part of a file management system with canvas/document sharing capabilities similar to Slack or other team collaboration tools.

This is a promotional modal dialog within Slack encouraging users to upgrade to a paid plan for unlimited canvas creation. The modal highlights premium collaboration tools and features like capturing notes, sharing across channels, and privacy controls. It appears over the main Slack messaging interface, with a clear call-to-action button to view upgrade options.

This is a Slackbot configuration modal within the Slack messaging platform. The dialog allows users to manage tabs for the Slackbot channel, including reordering, adding, removing, and hiding tabs like Messages, Files, and Pins. The interface shows typical Slack branding and provides options to add new tabs or canvas elements to customize the bot's functionality.

This is a Slack workspace interface showing the main messaging view with a purple-themed sidebar containing channels, direct messages, and navigation options. The interface displays command suggestions in a dropdown menu, including options for reminders, expanding/collapsing messages, and other Slack commands. A message composition area is visible at the bottom with formatting tools and a send button, along with a notification permission request banner.

This is a team collaboration platform interface showing a messaging workspace with channel-based communication. The left sidebar contains navigation for different sections like threads, huddles, drafts, directories, and channels including 'all-platotest', 'new-channel', and 'social'. The main content area displays a 'Test Folder' containing bookmarked items including website links and shared files. The interface supports file organization, bookmarking, and team communication features typical of workplace messaging platforms.

This is a Slack workspace file browser interface showing a sharing modal dialog overlay. The main interface displays a file management system with sidebar navigation for different file categories like All files, Canvases, Deleted, and Starred. The modal dialog allows users to share a canvas with team members, featuring permission settings (Can edit, Can view, Owner), direct messaging options, and a text area for adding messages. The interface demonstrates typical team collaboration features for file sharing within a communication platform.

This is a Stripe payment processing dashboard in sandbox/testing mode. The interface displays financial metrics with a line chart showing transaction amounts over time, along with sections for MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and Net Volume that are currently loading data. The left sidebar provides navigation to various payment-related features including transactions, customers, invoices, and billing management.

This is a configuration page for Stripe's revenue recovery features, specifically focused on email automation settings. The page allows users to configure automated emails for failed card payments, bank debit payment failures, and expiring credit cards. It includes toggle switches for enabling/disabling different email types and options for customizing the collection pages that customers are directed to when payment methods need updating.

This is an invoice creation and editing interface within Stripe's payment platform. The left side contains form fields for configuring invoice details like currency, items, quantities and pricing, while the right side shows a live preview of how the invoice will appear. The interface includes tabs for different output formats (PDF, Email, Payment page) and indicates this is a sandbox testing environment. Key features include auto-calculated totals, draft saving functionality, and the ability to add products from a catalog.

This is an export dialog overlay for payment transaction data in a Stripe sandbox environment. The modal allows users to configure export parameters including date range selection (today, current month, last 7 days, etc.) and column specifications. It displays a preview of the data fields that will be included in the export and provides cancel/export action buttons at the bottom.

This is a Stripe payment processing dashboard showing account balance overview in a sandbox environment. The interface displays a current balance of $31.57 with breakdown by payment types including incoming payments, available funds, and future refunds. The left sidebar provides navigation to various payment management features, while the main content area shows balance summaries, recent activity, and options to manage payouts and add bank accounts.

This is a customer update modal dialog within a payment processing platform (Stripe). The modal allows editing customer shipping information, including name and country selection from a dropdown list. The interface is overlaid on top of a dashboard showing customer details, payments, and subscription information. The left sidebar provides navigation to various payment management features like customers, transactions, billing, and reporting.

This is Stripe's Radar fraud prevention dashboard in sandbox mode, currently displaying the Rules tab. The interface features a comprehensive sidebar navigation with sections for core payment functionality, data management, and developer tools. The main content area shows a tabbed interface with sections for Overview, Reviews, Rules, Lists, Risk controls, and Insights, though the content appears to be in a loading state. A prominent banner indicates this is a testing sandbox environment with an option to switch to live account.

This is a Stripe dashboard showing the Sigma analytics tool in sandbox mode. The interface displays a SQL query editor for analyzing daily balance data, with a left sidebar containing various pre-built report templates organized into categories. The main content area shows a code editor with SQL syntax highlighting for creating custom financial reports and queries. Users can run queries to analyze payment and balance data with results displayed below the editor.

This is a payment method configuration page within Stripe's sandbox environment. Users can experiment with payment scenarios by entering transaction details, selecting location and currency, and configuring options like manual capture and future usage permissions. The interface includes a clear sidebar navigation and form-based controls for testing payment functionality before going live.

This is a Stripe dashboard showing a subscriptions management interface in sandbox mode. The page displays a data table of active subscriptions with customer information, billing modes, and status indicators. Users can filter subscriptions by status (Active, Scheduled, Canceled, etc.), create test subscriptions, and perform bulk operations like export and analysis. The interface includes typical SaaS dashboard elements like sidebar navigation for different business functions and tab-based organization of subscription-related features.

This is a Stripe product page showcasing terminal hardware and card readers in a sandbox environment. The page displays different card reader models with feature callouts like 'Does not require programming for operation'. A country selector dropdown is open showing delivery options to various countries. There's a notification about the S710 experiencing high demand and order delays.

This is a Stripe dashboard showing the Payment Links management interface in sandbox mode. The main content area displays a data table listing payment links with columns for name, price, and creation date. The sidebar provides navigation to various payment and business management features including balances, transactions, customers, and product catalog. The interface includes options to create new payment links, analyze data, and export information.

This is a Stripe payment platform dashboard showing a balance summary report in sandbox/testing mode. The interface displays account balance changes over time with filtering options for date ranges and timezone settings. The left sidebar provides navigation to various financial management features including transactions, customers, billing, and reporting tools. The main content area shows balance data in a tabular format with export and exploration capabilities.

This is a data table interface for viewing financial transactions in what appears to be Stripe's payment platform. The table shows columns for Date, Card ID, Cardholder ID, Dispute ID, and Type, with options to export data and edit columns. The interface is part of an 'Issuing' section within Stripe's dashboard, likely for managing card issuing transactions and disputes.

This is a Stripe payment processing dashboard showing customer account details and transaction history. The interface displays recent payment authorizations, connected cards, and dispute information in organized tables. The left sidebar provides navigation to various payment management features like transactions, customers, and billing tools. The right panel shows detailed customer information including billing address, contact details, and custom metadata fields.

This is a payment settings configuration page for Stripe's dashboard, specifically showing payment method settings for connected accounts. The interface displays various payment options like Cards, Cartes Bancaires, and Korean cards with their respective configurations. A modal dialog is overlaying the main content warning about unsaved changes, with options to either stay on the page or leave without saving modifications.

This is a settings page from Stripe's dashboard for configuring onboarding options for connected accounts. The page features a sidebar navigation, tabbed interface for different configuration categories, and a toggle switch to enable networked onboarding functionality. A preview panel on the right shows how the onboarding flow will appear to users, asking if they want to link accounts to existing businesses on Stripe.

This is a Stripe payment processing platform settings page for configuring site links. The page allows users to set up links for different components like Balances and Documents, with options to specify whether embedded components are used. It includes form validation, radio button selections, and a sidebar navigation menu with various payment-related sections like Transactions, Customers, and Billing.

This is a Stripe payment processing dashboard showing a transactions table in sandbox/testing mode. The interface displays payment records with details like amounts, status, currency, and customer information. A filter modal is currently open allowing users to filter transactions by status (pending, refunded, succeeded, etc.). The left sidebar provides navigation to various payment management features including balances, customers, and billing tools.

This is Walgreens' homepage featuring prominent Easter-themed promotional sections with discounts on seasonal items like candy, decorations, and gifts. The page displays multiple promotional banners offering 50% off deals and buy-one-get-one promotions across different product categories. Below the seasonal promotions is a Beauty Savings Event section highlighting cosmetics and personal care deals with rewards programs and percentage discounts.

This is a Walgreens pharmacy website homepage featuring a product showcase with health and beauty items displayed in a carousel format at the top. Below that is a promotional section offering up to $100 off with coupons, displaying manufacturer coupon offers in a grid layout. Each coupon card shows discount amounts ($3-$6 off 2 items) for brands like Garnier, L'Oreal Paris, Maybelline, and Dove with 'Clip' buttons to activate the offers. The layout emphasizes deals and promotions to encourage customer engagement and purchases.

This is the Walgreens homepage featuring a spring-themed promotional layout. The page displays coupon clips at the top, followed by an M&M's promotional banner offering buy 2 get 1 free deals. The main content area showcases 'Get prepped for spring' with four product category tiles featuring different health and wellness promotions including allergy relief, P&G oral care rewards, spring cleaning products, and vitamins. Each tile includes pricing, discount information, and product imagery with decorative spring flower elements.

This is Walgreens' main homepage featuring a health-focused design with the tagline 'Because your health matters'. The page displays four main service cards covering vaccination protection, flu/COVID-19 testing, weight-loss medication, and colon cancer screening. Below are additional service tiles for prescription management, refills, delivery, and telehealth consultations.
